content.full_text:
May 26, 1976

Mr. Randy Siefkin  
1225 Brady Avenue  
Modesto, California 95350

Dear Mr. Siefkin:

This letter is in response to your telephone inquiry regarding the filing obligations of Karen E. Johnson under the Political Reform Act.

You stated that Ms. Johnson filed her Declaration of Candidacy on February 11, 1975 in connection with an Assembly seat in the June 8 primary election and at that time paid her filing fee. She withdrew her name from the list of candidates by failure to file required signatures of her sponsors. She was never listed as a candidate on any official election material. You further stated that Ms. Johnson filed a campaign statement on April 29, 1976 covering the period from January 6, 1975 through April 26, 1976, listing all contributions received and expenditures made on behalf of her candidacy. You stated there have been no contributions received or expenditures made since April 26, 1976.

Ms. Johnson has fulfilled her campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act and if no additional money is received or expenditures made by June 30, 1976 (the closing date for off-year campaign statements), she may consider her April 29, 1976 report as her off-year campaign statement filed early.

Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

Sincerely,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

bcc: Jeanne  
RSK:mak

sections.conclusion: Ms. Johnson has fulfilled her campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act and if no additional money is received or expenditures made by June 30, 1976 (the closing date for off-year campaign statements), she may consider her April 29, 1976 report as her off-year campaign statement filed early.
sections.question_synthetic: What are Karen E. Johnson's campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act given that she filed a Declaration of Candidacy but withdrew before being officially listed as a candidate?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Ms. Johnson has satisfied her campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. Her April 29, 1976 campaign statement can serve as her off-year campaign statement if no additional contributions or expenditures occur by June 30, 1976.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question statement in opening paragraph and conclusion in body text. Response letter to telephone inquiry.
